conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You mentioned you used to enjoy walking the dog—what else has started to feel like a chore?
Cooking. I look at the recipe and just feel like I can't do it. I end up ordering takeout because it's just easier.
When you feel like you can't do something simple like cooking, what goes through your mind about yourself?
I think I’m losing myself. I used to be the one who could handle anything, and now I’m just tired all the time. It’s hard to accept that I’m not the same person.
What does it mean to you, not being the same person anymore?
It means I’m just going through the motions. I used to have goals and dreams, but now I just exist.
When you think about the future now, what comes to mind?
Nothing. I don’t know what I want anymore. I used to have a plan, but now I just don't see a point in making one.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
After the divorce. It was like the rug got pulled out from under me. I thought I had my life figured out, but now I just feel lost.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
The evenings. I used to look forward to coming home, but now the house just feels... empty.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. I just wake up, go through the motions, and wait for the day to be over. I don't have a plan for tomorrow, or next week, or next month.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
It’s both. I’m just so tired of fighting, and I don't know how much more of this I can do.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it harder to get excited about stuff you usually enjoy during the day?
Yeah, it does. I used to love hiking, but now I just can't seem to muster the energy to go.
When you're thinking about those things you could have done differently, what kind of feelings come up for you?
Just frustration, mostly. I keep thinking I should have seen it coming, but I didn't, so now I just feel like I'm stuck in this loop.
You mentioned feeling stuck in a loop—does that make it hard to see a way out of this feeling?
Yeah, it does. It feels like I'm just walking in circles, and I don't know how to stop it.
When you think about the future, does it feel like things could get better, or does it seem pretty stuck?
It seems pretty stuck. I don't know how to make it better, and that scares me because I don't want to be stuck in this place forever.
When you feel scared about being stuck like this, does it make you feel more on edge or irritable with people around you?
Yeah, definitely. I just get really short with people because I don't want them to see how broken I am right now.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
It started a few months ago. I was working on this big project, and it just fell apart, and after that, everything else just felt pointless too.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
Just everything. I used to love my job, but now it feels like I'm just going through the motions.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It’s just... I used to love it, but now it feels like I’m just ticking boxes, you know?
